policy.title: RBI Finalizes Climate Risk Disclosure Norms for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI issued final guidelines requiring all SCBs to integrate climate risk into ICAAP by March 2027, with mandatory physical and transition risk scenario analysis. Banks with assets over ₹5 lakh crore must submit first climate stress test results by Q2 FY27.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Top 500 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI mandated BRSR Core reporting with third-party assurance for top 500 companies by market cap from FY27, expanding from top 150. New requirements include Scope 3 emissions disclosure and water stress metrics by facility location.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Draft Framework for Index-Based Weather Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI circulated draft guidelines permitting general insurers to offer parametric products tied to IMD-certified weather indices for agricultural and MSME segments. Public comments invited until 30 April 2026.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Publishes Final Pillar 3 Climate Risk Disclosure Standard
policy.description: BCBS finalized Pillar 3 climate disclosure requirements with implementation from January 2028. Banks must disclose financed emissions for high-carbon sectors and physical risk exposures by geography using standardized templates.

policy.title: ISSB Releases Implementation Guidance for Emerging Market Jurisdictions
policy.description: IFRS Foundation published proportionality guidance allowing phased ISSB adoption for emerging markets, with simplified Scope 3 reporting in first two years. India's ICAI confirmed formal ISSB integration roadmap expected by June 2026.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: Updated scenarios include district-level physical hazard projections for South Asia and refined acute event probabilities. New variables include compound flooding and heat-humidity indices aligned with latest IPCC AR7 findings.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Partners with HDFC Bank for India Physical Risk Assessment
competitors.description: Jupiter Intelligence announced a strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to provide portfolio-level flood and cyclone risk scoring across 15,000+ commercial real estate assets. Deployment expected across HDFC's CRE lending book by Q3 2026.

competitors.title: MSCI Launches India Climate VaR Module for Banking Portfolios
competitors.description: MSCI ESG released a dedicated India Climate Value-at-Risk module covering 1,200 Indian corporates with physical and transition risk metrics. Product specifically targets RBI stress testing compliance requirements.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $12M Series A to Expand Supply Chain ESG Platform
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih closed Series A led by Accel India, with participation from existing investor Chiratae. Funds will expand supplier-level carbon accounting for textile and auto sectors serving export-oriented enterprises.

competitors.title: XDI Releases Gross Asset-Level Physical Risk API for APAC Banks
competitors.description: Cross Dependency Initiative launched an API product enabling real-time physical risk queries at individual asset coordinates, covering 8 perils across APAC. Pricing positioned for mid-market banks and NBFCs.

competitors.title: Moody's Partners with CRISIL for India SME Climate Scoring
competitors.description: Moody's ESG Solutions and CRISIL announced joint development of climate transition scores for Indian SMEs. Initial coverage targets 50,000 MSME borrowers in bank lending portfolios by end-2026.

competitors.title: CarbonMint Partners with Federal Bank for Agriculture Carbon Credits
competitors.description: CarbonMint signed MOU with Federal Bank to pilot blockchain-verified carbon credit issuance for 25,000 farmers in Kerala. Integration with bank's agri-lending platform planned for kharif 2026 season.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Banking: Stress Testing Approaches
research.description: RBI's internal working paper finds that acute flood events could increase NPAs by 45-80 bps in coastal state portfolios under moderate warming scenarios. Recommends granular asset-level hazard mapping for accurate PD calibration.

research.title: Parametric Insurance for Climate Resilience: Opportunities in South Asia
research.description: Swiss Re estimates India's parametric insurance market could reach $800M by 2030, driven by MSME and agriculture segments. Report identifies basis risk calibration and trigger transparency as key adoption barriers.

research.title: Climate-Related Financial Risks: Transmission Channels to Credit Risk
research.description: BIS working paper quantifies physical risk transmission to PD/LGD, finding that firms in high-flood-risk zones show 12-18% higher default probabilities. Proposes standardized framework for integrating hazard data into IRB models.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Challenges for Sustainable Finance
research.description: IFC analysis of 2,400 emerging market companies finds only 23% report Scope 1-2 emissions with third-party verification. Data gaps most acute in South Asia manufacturing and African agriculture sectors.

research.title: Landscape of Climate Finance in India 2025
research.description: CPI tracks ₹7.2 lakh crore in tracked climate finance for FY25, with adaptation receiving only 12% of flows. Report highlights critical need for physical risk data to unlock adaptation lending by commercial banks.

research.title: Natural Catastrophe Protection Gap: Asia-Pacific 2026
research.description: Munich Re estimates India's nat-cat protection gap at 92%, highest among major Asian economies. Recommends index-based products and public-private partnerships to close gap for flood and cyclone perils.

ourRead.headline: RBI's climate stress testing mandate creates a 12-month land grab for physical risk intelligence—and global players are already moving.
body: This week's signals converge on a single theme: Indian financial regulators are operationalizing climate risk requirements, and the competitive race to serve banks is intensifying. The RBI's finalized guidelines, SEBI's BRSR expansion, and IRDAI's parametric framework collectively signal that climate and ESG intelligence is transitioning from voluntary exploration to compliance infrastructure. For StepChange, the window to establish category leadership is narrowing.

The RBI's March 28 guidelines (policy-1) demanding climate stress test results by Q2 FY27 are the most consequential signal. Banks now have 14 months to operationalize physical risk scenario analysis with district-level granularity—exactly what the NGFS Phase V scenarios (policy-6) now provide. StepChange's PD/LGD modelling capability is precisely what SCBs need, but the mandate is explicit and urgent. RBI's own working paper (research-1) recommending 'granular asset-level hazard mapping' essentially validates our product thesis. We should be referencing this paper in every bank conversation.

Jupiter's HDFC partnership (comp-1) is a direct competitive threat—a global climate risk player securing India's largest private bank signals that incumbents won't wait for domestic solutions. MSCI's India Climate VaR module (comp-2) and Moody's-CRISIL SME scoring partnership (comp-5) show that FI data giants are localizing aggressively. However, these partnerships also reveal the market's size: if HDFC needs Jupiter and CRISIL needs Moody's, mid-tier banks and NBFCs—our core target—will need StepChange. The Sprih fundraise (comp-3) confirms continued investor appetite for India climate-tech, which supports our own capital narrative.

The BIS working paper (research-3) quantifying physical risk transmission to PD/LGD—with specific multipliers (12-18% higher default probability)—is immediately usable. We should integrate these coefficients into our sales materials and model documentation. Swiss Re's $800M parametric market estimate (research-2) and IRDAI's draft framework (policy-3) together validate Bet #2, but note Swiss Re's warning on basis risk calibration—our parametric product roadmap must address this explicitly.

Watch: The Basel Pillar 3 standard (policy-4) taking effect January 2028 will eventually cascade to Indian banks via RBI harmonization. Action: We need three signed bank pilots before Q3 2026 to establish reference customers ahead of the Jupiter/MSCI marketing push. Start with the RBI working paper as the conversation opener.
